case_profile.objective: Synthesize the scientific rationale and clinical evidence for BRAF V600E targeting in melanoma. Cover: (1) established clinical precedence for BRAF/MEK inhibitor combinations — dabrafenib plus trametinib, vemurafenib plus cobimetinib — distinguishing approved settings from ongoing questions about sequencing and durability; (2) adaptive and acquired resistance mechanisms including MAPK pathway reactivation via BRAF amplification, NRAS mutation, MEK1/2 mutation, and paradoxical ERK activation; (3) tumor microenvironment contributions to resistance and the rationale for immunotherapy combination — anti-PD-1, anti-LAG-3, and triplet strategies; (4) biomarkers predicting benefit versus intrinsic resistance including tumor mutational burden and immune infiltration signatures; and (5) validation experiments distinguishing MAPK-driven adaptive resistance from immune-mediated resistance that would inform combination trial design.

scientist_positions.position: BRAF V600E is a causally validated, clinically precedented oncogenic driver in melanoma with approved combination therapies; the outstanding scientific work concerns resistance biology, combination sequencing with immunotherapy, and biomarker-guided patient selection — not target discovery.
key_claims: BRAF V600E constitutively activates MAPK/ERK signaling, driving proliferation; dabrafenib+trametinib and vemurafenib+cobimetinib are FDA-approved with demonstrated OS benefit in BRAF V600E/K metastatic and adjuvant stage III melanoma
Acquired resistance converges on MAPK reactivation via BRAF amplification, splice variants, NRAS/MEK1/2 mutations, and RAS-independent paradoxical ERK activation through CRAF dimerization

Tumor microenvironment remodeling under BRAF inhibition transiently increases tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes and PD-L1, providing mechanistic rationale for BRAF/MEK + anti-PD-1 or anti-LAG-3 triplet strategies, though durability data remain incomplete
High TMB and inflamed immune gene expression signatures associate with immunotherapy benefit, while concurrent PTEN loss or high MITF amplification predict intrinsic resistance to targeted therapy
supporting_evidence_sources: NCBI Gene BRAF (gene_id 673): canonical MAPK/ERK pathway role and V600E as most frequent melanoma driver mutation
OpenTargets: BRAF association score 0.853 for melanoma; dabrafenib approval status confirmed across melanoma indications
PubMed PMID 37016119: real-world adjuvant dabrafenib+trametinib data in stage III BRAF-mut melanoma supporting clinical precedence
ClinicalTrials.gov records: active triplet and sequencing trials (BRAF/MEK + checkpoint inhibitor) confirming ongoing mechanistic and combination questions
concerns: Resistance is near-universal within 12–18 months for metastatic disease; no approved strategy durably overcomes MAPK reactivation-driven acquired resistance
Triplet BRAF/MEK + anti-PD-1 strategies (e.g., IMspire150) show modest PFS gains with additive toxicity; hepatotoxicity and pyrexia complicate combination tolerability
openFDA signals (basal cell carcinoma, ejection fraction decrease) reflect class-level paradoxical MAPK activation and cardiac liabilities requiring prospective monitoring
Real-world adjuvant data (PMID 37016119) are retrospective and single-center, limiting generalizability for sequencing decisions
requested_followups: Prospective biomarker-stratified trials distinguishing MAPK-driven vs. immune-mediated resistance at progression to guide second-line sequencing (targeted → immunotherapy vs. concurrent)
Functional validation of BRAF splice variant and MEK1/2 mutation co-occurrence as synthetic lethal vulnerabilities for next-generation ERK inhibitor combinations
Longitudinal TME profiling (scRNA-seq) during BRAF/MEK inhibition to define the optimal immunotherapy combination window before exhaustion sets in
Dedicated safety and PK studies for triplet regimens in patients with baseline cardiac dysfunction given ejection fraction decrease signals in openFDA

agreements: BRAF V600E is a causally validated, FDA-approved oncogenic driver in melanoma; this is not a new target discovery

Acquired resistance converges on MAPK reactivation via BRAF amplification, splice variants, NRAS/MEK1/2 mutations, and paradoxical ERK activation through CRAF dimerization
Transient TME remodeling under BRAF/MEK inhibition (increased TILs, PD-L1 upregulation) provides mechanistic rationale for triplet BRAF+MEK+checkpoint inhibitor strategies

disagreements: No substantive positional disagreements identified across agents; all three converge on support_with_limits with overlapping claims and concerns

Minor scope difference: mechanism_agent emphasizes functional validation of splice variant/MEK1/2 co-occurrence as synthetic lethal targets, while tooluniverse_agent prioritizes retrieving variant-level OpenTargets scores — these reflect disciplinary emphasis rather than scientific conflict
overclaims: Claim that TMB and immune infiltration signatures 'predict' benefit versus intrinsic resistance overstates current evidence; these are candidate biomarkers with associative, not prospectively validated predictive utility in BRAF-mutant melanoma specifically

Characterizing triplet BRAF+MEK+anti-PD-1 as having established 'rationale' from durability data is premature; IMspire150 showed modest PFS gain with additive toxicity and no confirmed OS benefit, making 'rationale' mechanistic only, not clinically validated

Median PFS of ~12 months cited for doublet BRAF+MEK without immunotherapy is a rough estimate that varies by trial and line of therapy; presenting it as a fixed benchmark without confidence intervals or trial context is imprecise
required_revisions: Biomarker claims (TMB, immune infiltration) must be qualified as associative/exploratory rather than validated predictive biomarkers until prospective stratified trial data are available

Triplet combination rationale must distinguish mechanistic rationale (TME remodeling data) from clinical validation (incomplete); IMspire150 and similar trials should be cited with specific endpoints and limitations
Resistance mechanism claims should specify whether evidence derives from in vitro models, patient biopsy series, or clinical trial correlatives, as the evidentiary weight differs substantially
Sequencing recommendations (targeted then immunotherapy vs. concurrent) must be explicitly flagged as unresolved and not implied as guideline-supported
Safety signals from openFDA (basal cell carcinoma, ejection fraction decrease) should be contextualized with incidence estimates where available rather than listed as qualitative liabilities only

accepted_claims: BRAF V600E constitutively activates MAPK/ERK signaling and is a causally validated, FDA-approved oncogenic driver in melanoma; this is not a new target discovery

Transient TME remodeling under BRAF/MEK inhibition (increased TILs, PD-L1 upregulation) provides mechanistic rationale for triplet BRAF+MEK+checkpoint inhibitor strategies
Real-world adjuvant data (PMID 37016119) support clinical precedence but are retrospective and single-center, limiting generalizability
softened_or_rejected_claims: TMB and immune infiltration signatures are candidate biomarkers associated with immunotherapy benefit but lack prospectively validated predictive utility specifically in BRAF-mutant melanoma; 'predict' overstates current evidence
Triplet BRAF+MEK+anti-PD-1 rationale remains mechanistic only; IMspire150 showed modest PFS gain with additive toxicity and no confirmed OS benefit, so clinical validation is incomplete
Median PFS ~12 months for doublet BRAF+MEK is an approximate benchmark that varies by trial and line of therapy and should not be cited as a fixed value without confidence intervals
PTEN loss and MITF amplification are associative markers of intrinsic resistance, not prospectively validated predictive biomarkers
pi_adjudication.revised_hypothesis: BRAF V600E is a causally validated, clinically precedented oncogenic driver in melanoma with FDA-approved combination therapies (dabrafenib+trametinib, vemurafenib+cobimetinib) demonstrating OS benefit in metastatic and adjuvant stage III settings. The outstanding scientific work concerns: (1) resistance biology — MAPK reactivation via BRAF amplification, splice variants, NRAS/MEK1/2 mutations, and paradoxical ERK activation are established mechanisms, but no approved strategy durably overcomes them; (2) combination sequencing with immunotherapy — transient TME remodeling provides mechanistic rationale for triplet strategies, but clinical validation of durability and tolerability remains incomplete; (3) biomarker-guided patient selection — TMB and immune infiltration signatures are candidate associative biomarkers for immunotherapy benefit, and PTEN loss/MITF amplification associate with targeted therapy resistance, but none are prospectively validated predictive biomarkers in this specific context. Priority experiments include prospective biomarker-stratified trials distinguishing MAPK-driven from immune-mediated resistance, longitudinal TME profiling to define optimal immunotherapy combination windows, and dedicated safety studies for triplet regimens in patients with baseline cardiac dysfunction.
pi_adjudication.confidence_adjustment: Maintained with minor softening on biomarker predictive claims and triplet clinical validation; no upward adjustment warranted given incomplete OS data for triplets and associative-only biomarker evidence
pi_adjudication.rationale: All three agents converge on support_with_limits with no substantive positional disagreements. Core claims regarding target validation, approved combinations, and resistance mechanisms are well-supported by regulatory records, OpenTargets scores, and published literature. Confidence is held below 0.85 because: biomarker claims are associative not prospectively validated; triplet strategies lack confirmed OS benefit; and the primary real-world evidence source is retrospective and single-center. The revised hypothesis preserves all supported mechanistic and clinical claims while enforcing appropriate epistemic guardrails on biomarker and combination durability assertions.

output.critique: 1) OS benefit claim for adjuvant stage III is overstated: COMBI-AD showed RFS/DMFS benefit but OS data remain immature; asserting OS benefit conflates surrogate and hard endpoints. 2) Resistance mechanisms listed as 'established' rely heavily on preclinical and small retrospective series; prospective mechanistic validation in human tumors is incomplete. 3) TME remodeling rationale for triplet immunotherapy combinations is extrapolated from short-window biopsy studies and mouse models; durability claims lack Phase III support. 4) TMB and immune infiltration described as 'candidate associative biomarkers' but the evidence digest contains no prospectively validated predictive biomarker study in this specific BRAF-mutant melanoma context. 5) MAP2K7/JNK pathway evidence is irrelevant to BRAF V600E-MEK1/2-ERK axis and inflates apparent mechanistic support. 6) Safety gap: cardiac dysfunction subgroup for triplet regimens is flagged as a priority but openFDA signals (ejection fraction decrease, angina) are not quantified or contextualized against baseline cardiac risk in trial populations.
output.recommended_fix: 1) Replace 'OS benefit' with 'RFS/DMFS benefit with OS data pending maturity' for adjuvant setting. 2) Qualify resistance mechanisms as 'preclinically and retrospectively characterized, prospective validation lacking.' 3) Restrict TME combination rationale to mechanistic hypothesis tier, not clinical validation. 4) Remove MAP2K7 evidence or explicitly label it as off-pathway. 5) Add explicit prospective biomarker trial citation requirement before claiming predictive biomarker candidacy. 6) Quantify cardiac AE rates from trial publications rather than relying on raw FAERS counts.
output.abstention_required: partial: OS benefit in adjuvant setting cannot be confirmed without mature OS data; predictive biomarker claims require abstention pending prospective validation
output.claim_boundary: Supported: BRAF V600E as oncogenic driver, FDA approval of doublet regimens, RFS benefit in adjuvant stage III, mechanistic resistance taxonomy. Not supported: durable OS benefit in adjuvant setting, prospectively validated predictive biomarkers, clinical durability of triplet immunotherapy combinations.

The outstanding scientific work concerns: (1) resistance biology — MAPK reactivation via BRAF amplification, splice variants, NRAS/MEK1/2 mutations, and paradoxical ERK activation are established mechanisms, but no approved strategy durably overcomes them; (2) combination sequencing with immunotherapy — transient TME remodeling provides mechanistic rationale for triplet strategies, but clinical validation of durability and tolerability remains incomplete; (3) biomarker-guided patient selection — TMB and immune infiltration signatures are candidate associative biomarkers for immunotherapy benefit, and PTEN loss/MITF amplification associate with targeted therapy resistance, but none are prospectively validated predictive biomarkers in this specific context. evidence.text: NCBI Gene BRAF: This gene encodes a protein belonging to the RAF family of serine/threonine protein kinases. This protein plays a role in regulating the MAP kinase/ERK signaling pathway, which affects cell division, differentiation, and secretion. Mutations in this gene, most commonly the V600E mutation, are the most frequently identified cancer-causing mutations in melanoma, and have been identified in various other cancers as well, including non-Hodgkin lymphoma, colorectal cancer, thyroid carcinoma, non-small cell lung carcinoma, hairy cell leukemia and adenocarcinoma of lung. Mutations in this gene are also associated with cardiofaciocutaneous, Noonan, and Costello syndromes, which exhibit overlapping phenotypes. A pseudogene of this gene has been identified on the X chromosome. [provided by RefSeq, Aug 2017]

Mutations in this gene are also associated with cardiofaciocutaneous, Noonan, and Costello syndromes, which exhibit overlapping phenotypes. evidence.text: NCBI Gene MAP2K7: The protein encoded by this gene is a dual specificity protein kinase that belongs to the MAP kinase kinase family. This kinase specifically activates MAPK8/JNK1 and MAPK9/JNK2, and this kinase itself is phosphorylated and activated by MAP kinase kinase kinases including MAP3K1/MEKK1, MAP3K2/MEKK2,MAP3K3/MEKK5, and MAP4K2/GCK. This kinase is involved in the signal transduction mediating the cell responses to proinflammatory cytokines, and environmental stresses. Alternative splicing results in multiple transcript variants. [provided by RefSeq, Jul 2014]

evidence.text: OpenTargets_get_disease_id_description_by_name returned OpenTargets search hits: melanoma: A malignant, usually aggressive tumor composed of atypical, neoplastic melanocytes. Most often, melanomas arise in the skin (cutaneous melanomas) and include the following histologic subtypes: superficial spreading melanoma, nodular melanoma, acral lentiginous melanoma, and lentigo maligna melanoma. Cutaneous melanomas may arise from acquired or congenital melanocytic or dysplastic nevi. Melanomas may also arise in other anatomic sites including the gastrointestinal system, eye, urinary tract, and reproductive system. Melanomas frequently metastasize to lymph nodes, liver, lungs, and brain.; cutaneous melanoma: A primary melanoma arising from atypical melanocytes in the skin. Precursor lesions include acquired and congenital melanocytic nevi, and dysplastic nevi. Several histologic variants have been recognized, including superficial spreading melanoma, acral lentiginous melanoma, nodular melanoma, and lentigo maligna melanoma.; Uveal Melanoma: A melanoma derived from melanocytes of the uveal tract. It is the most common primary intraocular tumor in the United States and Western Europe. Similar to melanoma of the skin, it is rare in Africa and Asia. Diagnostic procedures include ophthalmoscopic exam, fluorescein angiography and ultrasound. Treatment includes: surgical excision of the eye, iridocyclectomy and tumor resection. Recent treatments also include radiotherapy or photo coagulation. Classification of uveal melanomas recognizes four cell types within these tumors: epithelioid, intermediate, mixed cell, and spindle cell types. The spindle cell type uveal melanomas are further sub-classified as spindle cell type A and spindle cell type B.

Melanomas may also arise in other anatomic sites including the gastrointestinal system, eye, urinary tract, and reproductive system. Melanomas frequently metastasize to lymph nodes, liver, lungs, and brain.

hits.name: cutaneous melanoma
hits.description: A primary melanoma arising from atypical melanocytes in the skin. Precursor lesions include acquired and congenital melanocytic nevi, and dysplastic nevi. Several histologic variants have been recognized, including superficial spreading melanoma, acral lentiginous melanoma, nodular melanoma, and lentigo maligna melanoma.

hits.name: Uveal Melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma derived from melanocytes of the uveal tract. It is the most common primary intraocular tumor in the United States and Western Europe. Similar to melanoma of the skin, it is rare in Africa and Asia. Diagnostic procedures include ophthalmoscopic exam, fluorescein angiography and ultrasound. Treatment includes: surgical excision of the eye, iridocyclectomy and tumor resection. Recent treatments also include radiotherapy or photo coagulation. Classification of uveal melanomas recognizes four cell types within these tumors: epithelioid, intermediate, mixed cell, and spindle cell types. The spindle cell type uveal melanomas are further sub-classified as spindle cell type A and spindle cell type B.

hits.name: melanoma-derived growth regulatory protein  level
hits.description: The amount of a melanoma-derived growth regulatory protein when measured in anatomical entity.

hits.name: superficial spreading melanoma
hits.description: A type of melanoma that typically occurs in light-skinned individuals ranging in age from young adults to the elderly. Risk factors include extensive sun exposure during childhood, a family history of melanoma, and the presence of dysplastic nevi.

hits.name: nodular melanoma
hits.description: An aggressive form of melanoma, frequently metastasizing to the lymph nodes. It presents as a papular or nodular raised skin lesion. It comprises approximately 10-15% of melanomas. Morphologically, it often displays an epithelioid appearance.

hits.name: retinal melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma affecting the retinal portion of the eye. --2003

hits.name: melanoma, cutaneous malignant, susceptibility to, 8
hits.description: An inherited cancer-predisposing syndrome due to a gain-of-function germline mutation in the MITF gene, associated with a higher incidence of amelanotic and nodular melanoma, multiple primary melanomas and increase in nevus number and size. It may also predispose to co-occurring melanoma and renal cell carcinoma and to pancreatic cancer.

hits.name: esophageal melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma affecting the esophageal wall. Melanoma in the esophagus is more commonly metastatic than primary. Primary melanomas of the esophagus are polypoid and clinically aggressive. (WHO, 2000)

hits.name: Non-Cutaneous Melanoma
hits.description: Melanoma is a malignant tumor of melanocytes, cells that are derived from the neural crest. Although most melanomas arise in the skin, they also may arise from mucosal surfaces or at other sites to which neural crest cells migrate. (PDQ)

hits.name: Ocular Melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma that arises from the structures of the eye or ocular adnexa.

hits.name: Spindle Cell Melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma characterized by the presence of malignant spindle-shaped melanocytes.

hits.name: acral lentiginous melanoma
hits.description: A form of melanoma occurring most often on the plantar, palmar, subungual, and periungual skin. It presents as a pigmented macular lesion with irregular borders. Morphologically, it consists of atypical spindled and dendritic melanocytes. The epidermis is often hyperplastic and there is pagetoid infiltration of the epidermis by anaplastic cells.

hits.name: digestive system melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma that arises from any part of the digestive system.

hits.name: level of melanoma-associated antigen B10 in blood serum
hits.description: The amount of a melanoma-associated antigen B10 when measured in blood serum.

hits.name: amelanotic melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma characterized by the complete absence of melanin pigment in the melanoma cells. It occurs more frequently on the face and it is often associated with desmoplastic reaction.

hits.name: Mixed Cell Uveal Melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma arising from the choroid, ciliary body, or the iris. It is characterized by the presence of a mixture of spindle A melanoma cells, spindle B melanoma cells, and epithelioid melanoma cells.

hits.name: Anal Melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma arising from the anus. Clinical presentation includes rectal bleeding, tenesmus, pain, and change in bowel habit. The prognosis is usually poor.

hits.name: choroid spindle cell melanoma
hits.description: A spindle cell melanoma that involves the optic choroid.

hits.name: metastatic melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma that has spread from its primary site to another anatomic site. Melanomas frequently metastasize to lymph nodes, liver, lungs, and brain.

hits.name: amelanotic skin melanoma
hits.description: An amelanotic melanoma that involves the zone of skin.

hits.name: Conjunctival Melanoma
hits.description: A malignant melanoma within the conjunctiva of the eye.

hits.name: desmoplastic melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma of the skin characterized by a proliferation of atypical spindled melanocytes in the dermis, in a background of abundant collagen. It usually presents as an amelanotic raised nodular lesion.

hits.name: Epithelioid Cell Uveal Melanoma
hits.description: A uveal melanoma characterized by the presence of malignant large epithelioid melanocytes.

hits.name: spindle cell intraocular melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma arising from the choroid, ciliary body, or the iris. It is characterized by the presence of spindle-shaped melanocytes.

TMB and immune infiltration signatures are candidate biomarkers associated with immunotherapy benefit but lack prospectively validated predictive utility specifically in BRAF-mutant melanoma; 'predict' overstates current evidence
Triplet BRAF+MEK+anti-PD-1 rationale remains mechanistic only; IMspire150 showed modest PFS gain with additive toxicity and no confirmed OS benefit, so clinical validation is incomplete
Median PFS ~12 months for doublet BRAF+MEK is an approximate benchmark that varies by trial and line of therapy and should not be cited as a fixed value without confidence intervals
PTEN loss and MITF amplification are associative markers of intrinsic resistance, not prospectively validated predictive biomarkers

case_profile.objective: Synthesize the scientific rationale and clinical evidence for BRAF V600E targeting in melanoma. Cover: (1) established clinical precedence for BRAF/MEK inhibitor combinations — dabrafenib plus trametinib, vemurafenib plus cobimetinib — distinguishing approved settings from ongoing questions about sequencing and durability; (2) adaptive and acquired resistance mechanisms including MAPK pathway reactivation via BRAF amplification, NRAS mutation, MEK1/2 mutation, and paradoxical ERK activation; (3) tumor microenvironment contributions to resistance and the rationale for immunotherapy combination — anti-PD-1, anti-LAG-3, and triplet strategies; (4) biomarkers predicting benefit versus intrinsic resistance including tumor mutational burden and immune infiltration signatures; and (5) validation experiments distinguishing MAPK-driven adaptive resistance from immune-mediated resistance that would inform combination trial design.

pi_adjudication.revised_hypothesis: BRAF V600E is a causally validated, clinically precedented oncogenic driver in melanoma with FDA-approved combination therapies (dabrafenib+trametinib, vemurafenib+cobimetinib) demonstrating OS benefit in metastatic and adjuvant stage III settings. The outstanding scientific work concerns: (1) resistance biology — MAPK reactivation via BRAF amplification, splice variants, NRAS/MEK1/2 mutations, and paradoxical ERK activation are established mechanisms, but no approved strategy durably overcomes them; (2) combination sequencing with immunotherapy — transient TME remodeling provides mechanistic rationale for triplet strategies, but clinical validation of durability and tolerability remains incomplete; (3) biomarker-guided patient selection — TMB and immune infiltration signatures are candidate associative biomarkers for immunotherapy benefit, and PTEN loss/MITF amplification associate with targeted therapy resistance, but none are prospectively validated predictive biomarkers in this specific context. Priority experiments include prospective biomarker-stratified trials distinguishing MAPK-driven from immune-mediated resistance, longitudinal TME profiling to define optimal immunotherapy combination windows, and dedicated safety studies for triplet regimens in patients with baseline cardiac dysfunction.
pi_adjudication.confidence_adjustment: Maintained with minor softening on biomarker predictive claims and triplet clinical validation; no upward adjustment warranted given incomplete OS data for triplets and associative-only biomarker evidence
pi_adjudication.rationale: All three agents converge on support_with_limits with no substantive positional disagreements. Core claims regarding target validation, approved combinations, and resistance mechanisms are well-supported by regulatory records, OpenTargets scores, and published literature. Confidence is held below 0.85 because: biomarker claims are associative not prospectively validated; triplet strategies lack confirmed OS benefit; and the primary real-world evidence source is retrospective and single-center. The revised hypothesis preserves all supported mechanistic and clinical claims while enforcing appropriate epistemic guardrails on biomarker and combination durability assertions.

OpenTargets_get_disease_id_description_by_name returned OpenTargets search hits: melanoma: A malignant, usually aggressive tumor composed of atypical, neoplastic melanocytes. Most often, melanomas arise in the skin (cutaneous melanomas) and include the following histologic subtypes: superficial spreading melanoma, nodular melanoma, acral lentiginous melanoma, and lentigo maligna melanoma. Cutaneous melanomas may arise from acquired or congenital melanocytic or dysplastic nevi. Melanomas may also arise in other anatomic sites including the gastrointestinal system, eye, urinary tract, and reproductive system. Melanomas frequently metastasize to lymph nodes, liver, lungs, and brain.; cutaneous

critique.critique: 1) OS benefit claim for adjuvant stage III is overstated: COMBI-AD showed RFS/DMFS benefit but OS data remain immature; asserting OS benefit conflates surrogate and hard endpoints. 2) Resistance mechanisms listed as 'established' rely heavily on preclinical and small retrospective series; prospective mechanistic validation in human tumors is incomplete. 3) TME remodeling rationale for triplet immunotherapy combinations is extrapolated from short-window biopsy studies and mouse models; durability claims lack Phase III support. 4) TMB and immune infiltration described as 'candidate associative biomarkers' but the evidence digest contains no prospectively validated predictive biomarker study in this specific BRAF-mutant melanoma context. 5) MAP2K7/JNK pathway evidence is irrelevant to BRAF V600E-MEK1/2-ERK axis and inflates apparent mechanistic support. 6) Safety gap: cardiac dysfunction subgroup for triplet regimens is flagged as a priority but openFDA signals (ejection fraction decrease, angina) are not quantified or contextualized against baseline cardiac risk in trial populations.
critique.recommended_fix: 1) Replace 'OS benefit' with 'RFS/DMFS benefit with OS data pending maturity' for adjuvant setting. 2) Qualify resistance mechanisms as 'preclinically and retrospectively characterized, prospective validation lacking.' 3) Restrict TME combination rationale to mechanistic hypothesis tier, not clinical validation. 4) Remove MAP2K7 evidence or explicitly label it as off-pathway. 5) Add explicit prospective biomarker trial citation requirement before claiming predictive biomarker candidacy. 6) Quantify cardiac AE rates from trial publications rather than relying on raw FAERS counts.
critique.abstention_required: partial: OS benefit in adjuvant setting cannot be confirmed without mature OS data; predictive biomarker claims require abstention pending prospective validation
critique.claim_boundary: Supported: BRAF V600E as oncogenic driver, FDA approval of doublet regimens, RFS benefit in adjuvant stage III, mechanistic resistance taxonomy. Not supported: durable OS benefit in adjuvant setting, prospectively validated predictive biomarkers, clinical durability of triplet immunotherapy combinations.

{
  "title": "BRAF V600E as a Clinically Precedented Oncogenic Driver in Metastatic and Adjuvant Melanoma",
  "summary": "BRAF V600E is a causally validated, clinically precedented oncogenic driver in melanoma, w

hypothesis_card.title: BRAF clinical-precedence and mechanism review for melanoma
hypothesis_card.hypothesis: BRAF V600E is a causally validated, clinically precedented oncogenic driver in melanoma with FDA-approved combination therapies (dabrafenib+trametinib, vemurafenib+cobimetinib) demonstrating OS benefit in metastatic and adjuvant stage III settings. The outstanding scientific work concerns: (1) resistance biology — MAPK reactivation via BRAF amplification, splice variants, NRAS/MEK1/2 mutations, and paradoxical ERK activation are established mechanisms, but no approved strategy durably overcomes them; (2) combination sequencing with immunotherapy — transient TME remodeling provides mechanistic rationale for triplet strategies, but clinical validation of durability and tolerability remains incomplete; (3) biomarker-guided patient selection — TMB and immune infiltration signatures are candidate associative biomarkers for immunotherapy benefit, and PTEN loss/MITF amplification associate with targeted therapy resistance, but none are prospectively validated predictive biomarkers in this specific context. Priority experiments include prospective biomarker-stratified trials distinguishing MAPK-driven from immune-mediated resistance, longitudinal TME profiling to define optimal immunotherapy combination windows, and dedicated safety studies for triplet regimens in patients with baseline cardiac dysfunction.

evidence.text: NCBI Gene BRAF: This gene encodes a protein belonging to the RAF family of serine/threonine protein kinases. This protein plays a role in regulating the MAP kinase/ERK signaling pathway, which affects cell division, differentiation, and secretion. Mutations in this gene, most commonly the V600E mutation, are the most frequently identified cancer-causing mutations in melanoma, and have been identified in various other cancers as well, including non-Hodgkin lymphoma, colorectal cancer, thyroid carcinoma, non-small cell lung carcinoma, hairy cell leukemia and adenocarcinoma of lung. Mutations in this gene are also associated with cardiofaciocutaneous, Noonan, and Costello syndromes, which exhibit overlapping phenotypes. A pseudogene of this gene has been identified on the X chromosome. [provided by RefSeq, Aug 2017]

Mutations in this gene are also associated with cardiofaciocutaneous, Noonan, and Costello syndromes, which exhibit overlapping phenotypes. Cutaneous melanomas may arise from acquired or congenital melanocytic or dysplastic nevi. Melanomas may also arise in other anatomic sites including the gastrointestinal system, eye, urinary tract, and reproductive system. Melanomas frequently metastasize to lymph nodes, liver, lungs, and brain.; cutaneous melanoma: A primary melanoma arising from atypical melanocytes in the skin. Precursor lesions include acquired and congenital melanocytic nevi, and dysplastic nevi. Several histologic variants have been recognized, including superficial spreading melanoma, acral lentiginous melanoma, nodular melanoma, and lentigo maligna melanoma.; Uveal Melanoma: A melanoma derived from melanocytes of the uveal tract. It is the most common primary intraocular tumor in the United States and Western Europe. Similar to melanoma of the skin, it is rare in Africa and Asia. Diagnostic procedures include ophthalmoscopic exam, fluorescein angiography and ultrasound. Treatment includes: surgical excision of the eye, iridocyclectomy and tumor resection. Recent treatments also include radiotherapy or photo coagulation. Classification of uveal melanomas recognizes four cell types within these tumors: epithelioid, intermediate, mixed cell, and spindle cell types. The spindle cell type uveal melanomas are further sub-classified as spindle cell type A and spindle cell type B.

hits.name: melanoma-derived growth regulatory protein  level
hits.description: The amount of a melanoma-derived growth regulatory protein when measured in anatomical entity.

hits.name: superficial spreading melanoma
hits.description: A type of melanoma that typically occurs in light-skinned individuals ranging in age from young adults to the elderly. Risk factors include extensive sun exposure during childhood, a family history of melanoma, and the presence of dysplastic nevi.

hits.name: nodular melanoma
hits.description: An aggressive form of melanoma, frequently metastasizing to the lymph nodes. It presents as a papular or nodular raised skin lesion. It comprises approximately 10-15% of melanomas. Morphologically, it often displays an epithelioid appearance.

hits.name: retinal melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma affecting the retinal portion of the eye. --2003

hits.name: melanoma, cutaneous malignant, susceptibility to, 8
hits.description: An inherited cancer-predisposing syndrome due to a gain-of-function germline mutation in the MITF gene, associated with a higher incidence of amelanotic and nodular melanoma, multiple primary melanomas and increase in nevus number and size. It may also predispose to co-occurring melanoma and renal cell carcinoma and to pancreatic cancer.

hits.name: esophageal melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma affecting the esophageal wall. Melanoma in the esophagus is more commonly metastatic than primary. Primary melanomas of the esophagus are polypoid and clinically aggressive. (WHO, 2000)

hits.name: Non-Cutaneous Melanoma
hits.description: Melanoma is a malignant tumor of melanocytes, cells that are derived from the neural crest. Although most melanomas arise in the skin, they also may arise from mucosal surfaces or at other sites to which neural crest cells migrate. (PDQ)

hits.name: Ocular Melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma that arises from the structures of the eye or ocular adnexa.

hits.name: Spindle Cell Melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma characterized by the presence of malignant spindle-shaped melanocytes.

hits.name: acral lentiginous melanoma
hits.description: A form of melanoma occurring most often on the plantar, palmar, subungual, and periungual skin. It presents as a pigmented macular lesion with irregular borders. Morphologically, it consists of atypical spindled and dendritic melanocytes. The epidermis is often hyperplastic and there is pagetoid infiltration of the epidermis by anaplastic cells.

hits.name: digestive system melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma that arises from any part of the digestive system.

hits.name: level of melanoma-associated antigen B10 in blood serum
hits.description: The amount of a melanoma-associated antigen B10 when measured in blood serum.

hits.name: amelanotic melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma characterized by the complete absence of melanin pigment in the melanoma cells. It occurs more frequently on the face and it is often associated with desmoplastic reaction.

hits.name: Mixed Cell Uveal Melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma arising from the choroid, ciliary body, or the iris. It is characterized by the presence of a mixture of spindle A melanoma cells, spindle B melanoma cells, and epithelioid melanoma cells.

hits.name: Anal Melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma arising from the anus. Clinical presentation includes rectal bleeding, tenesmus, pain, and change in bowel habit. The prognosis is usually poor.

hits.name: choroid spindle cell melanoma
hits.description: A spindle cell melanoma that involves the optic choroid.

hits.name: metastatic melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma that has spread from its primary site to another anatomic site. Melanomas frequently metastasize to lymph nodes, liver, lungs, and brain.

hits.name: amelanotic skin melanoma
hits.description: An amelanotic melanoma that involves the zone of skin.

hits.name: Conjunctival Melanoma
hits.description: A malignant melanoma within the conjunctiva of the eye.

hits.name: desmoplastic melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma of the skin characterized by a proliferation of atypical spindled melanocytes in the dermis, in a background of abundant collagen. It usually presents as an amelanotic raised nodular lesion.

hits.name: Epithelioid Cell Uveal Melanoma
hits.description: A uveal melanoma characterized by the presence of malignant large epithelioid melanocytes.

hits.name: spindle cell intraocular melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma arising from the choroid, ciliary body, or the iris. It is characterized by the presence of spindle-shaped melanocytes.

case_profile.objective: Synthesize the scientific rationale and clinical evidence for BRAF V600E targeting in melanoma. Cover: (1) established clinical precedence for BRAF/MEK inhibitor combinations — dabrafenib plus trametinib, vemurafenib plus cobimetinib — distinguishing approved settings from ongoing questions about sequencing and durability; (2) adaptive and acquired resistance mechanisms including MAPK pathway reactivation via BRAF amplification, NRAS mutation, MEK1/2 mutation, and paradoxical ERK activation; (3) tumor microenvironment contributions to resistance and the rationale for immunotherapy combination — anti-PD-1, anti-LAG-3, and triplet strategies; (4) biomarkers predicting benefit versus intrinsic resistance including tumor mutational burden and immune infiltration signatures; and (5) validation experiments distinguishing MAPK-driven adaptive resistance from immune-mediated resistance that would inform combination trial design.

scientist_positions.position: BRAF V600E is a causally validated, clinically precedented oncogenic driver in melanoma with approved combination therapies; the outstanding scientific work concerns resistance biology, combination sequencing with immunotherapy, and biomarker-guided patient selection — not target discovery.
key_claims: BRAF V600E constitutively activates MAPK/ERK signaling, driving proliferation; dabrafenib+trametinib and vemurafenib+cobimetinib are FDA-approved with demonstrated OS benefit in BRAF V600E/K metastatic and adjuvant stage III melanoma
Acquired resistance converges on MAPK reactivation via BRAF amplification, splice variants, NRAS/MEK1/2 mutations, and RAS-independent paradoxical ERK activation through CRAF dimerization

Tumor microenvironment remodeling under BRAF inhibition transiently increases tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes and PD-L1, providing mechanistic rationale for BRAF/MEK + anti-PD-1 or anti-LAG-3 triplet strategies, though durability data remain incomplete
High TMB and inflamed immune gene expression signatures associate with immunotherapy benefit, while concurrent PTEN loss or high MITF amplification predict intrinsic resistance to targeted therapy
supporting_evidence_sources: NCBI Gene BRAF (gene_id 673): canonical MAPK/ERK pathway role and V600E as most frequent melanoma driver mutation
OpenTargets: BRAF association score 0.853 for melanoma; dabrafenib approval status confirmed across melanoma indications
PubMed PMID 37016119: real-world adjuvant dabrafenib+trametinib data in stage III BRAF-mut melanoma supporting clinical precedence
ClinicalTrials.gov records: active triplet and sequencing trials (BRAF/MEK + checkpoint inhibitor) confirming ongoing mechanistic and combination questions
concerns: Resistance is near-universal within 12–18 months for metastatic disease; no approved strategy durably overcomes MAPK reactivation-driven acquired resistance
Triplet BRAF/MEK + anti-PD-1 strategies (e.g., IMspire150) show modest PFS gains with additive toxicity; hepatotoxicity and pyrexia complicate combination tolerability
openFDA signals (basal cell carcinoma, ejection fraction decrease) reflect class-level paradoxical MAPK activation and cardiac liabilities requiring prospective monitoring
Real-world adjuvant data (PMID 37016119) are retrospective and single-center, limiting generalizability for sequencing decisions
requested_followups: Prospective biomarker-stratified trials distinguishing MAPK-driven vs. immune-mediated resistance at progression to guide second-line sequencing (targeted → immunotherapy vs. concurrent)
Functional validation of BRAF splice variant and MEK1/2 mutation co-occurrence as synthetic lethal vulnerabilities for next-generation ERK inhibitor combinations
Longitudinal TME profiling (scRNA-seq) during BRAF/MEK inhibition to define the optimal immunotherapy combination window before exhaustion sets in
Dedicated safety and PK studies for triplet regimens in patients with baseline cardiac dysfunction given ejection fraction decrease signals in openFDA

pi_adjudication.revised_hypothesis: BRAF V600E is a causally validated, clinically precedented oncogenic driver in melanoma with FDA-approved combination therapies (dabrafenib+trametinib, vemurafenib+cobimetinib) demonstrating OS benefit in metastatic and adjuvant stage III settings. The outstanding scientific work concerns: (1) resistance biology — MAPK reactivation via BRAF amplification, splice variants, NRAS/MEK1/2 mutations, and paradoxical ERK activation are established mechanisms, but no approved strategy durably overcomes them; (2) combination sequencing with immunotherapy — transient TME remodeling provides mechanistic rationale for triplet strategies, but clinical validation of durability and tolerability remains incomplete; (3) biomarker-guided patient selection — TMB and immune infiltration signatures are candidate associative biomarkers for immunotherapy benefit, and PTEN loss/MITF amplification associate with targeted therapy resistance, but none are prospectively validated predictive biomarkers in this specific context. Priority experiments include prospective biomarker-stratified trials distinguishing MAPK-driven from immune-mediated resistance, longitudinal TME profiling to define optimal immunotherapy combination windows, and dedicated safety studies for triplet regimens in patients with baseline cardiac dysfunction.
pi_adjudication.confidence_adjustment: Maintained with minor softening on biomarker predictive claims and triplet clinical validation; no upward adjustment warranted given incomplete OS data for triplets and associative-only biomarker evidence
pi_adjudication.rationale: All three agents converge on support_with_limits with no substantive positional disagreements. Core claims regarding target validation, approved combinations, and resistance mechanisms are well-supported by regulatory records, OpenTargets scores, and published literature. Confidence is held below 0.85 because: biomarker claims are associative not prospectively validated; triplet strategies lack confirmed OS benefit; and the primary real-world evidence source is retrospective and single-center. The revised hypothesis preserves all supported mechanistic and clinical claims while enforcing appropriate epistemic guardrails on biomarker and combination durability assertions.

key_claims: BRAF V600E is the most frequently identified oncogenic mutation in melanoma and encodes a constitutively active serine/threonine kinase that drives MAP kinase/ERK pathway signaling, as established by NCBI Gene annotation and an Open Targets disease-association score of 0.853.

Dabrafenib (approved for melanoma across multiple indications per Open Targets) and trametinib are FDA-approved agents targeting the BRAF V600E-MEK-ERK axis, with adjuvant dabrafenib plus trametinib demonstrating relapse-free survival and distant metastasis-free survival benefit in stage III BRAF-mutant melanoma; mature overall survival data are not yet available.

Acquired resistance to BRAF-targeted therapy involves preclinically and retrospectively characterized MAPK reactivation mechanisms—including BRAF amplification, splice variants, and NRAS/MEK1/2 mutations—but prospective mechanistic validation in human tumor cohorts remains incomplete.

PTEN loss is associated with resistance to BRAF-targeted therapy in melanoma based on available evidence, but neither PTEN loss nor TMB nor immune infiltration signatures have been prospectively validated as predictive biomarkers for treatment selection in BRAF-mutant melanoma.

critique.critique: 1) OS benefit claim for adjuvant stage III is overstated: COMBI-AD showed RFS/DMFS benefit but OS data remain immature; asserting OS benefit conflates surrogate and hard endpoints. 2) Resistance mechanisms listed as 'established' rely heavily on preclinical and small retrospective series; prospective mechanistic validation in human tumors is incomplete. 3) TME remodeling rationale for triplet immunotherapy combinations is extrapolated from short-window biopsy studies and mouse models; durability claims lack Phase III support. 4) TMB and immune infiltration described as 'candidate associative biomarkers' but the evidence digest contains no prospectively validated predictive biomarker study in this specific BRAF-mutant melanoma context. 5) MAP2K7/JNK pathway evidence is irrelevant to BRAF V600E-MEK1/2-ERK axis and inflates apparent mechanistic support. 6) Safety gap: cardiac dysfunction subgroup for triplet regimens is flagged as a priority but openFDA signals (ejection fraction decrease, angina) are not quantified or contextualized against baseline cardiac risk in trial populations.
critique.recommended_fix: 1) Replace 'OS benefit' with 'RFS/DMFS benefit with OS data pending maturity' for adjuvant setting. 2) Qualify resistance mechanisms as 'preclinically and retrospectively characterized, prospective validation lacking.' 3) Restrict TME combination rationale to mechanistic hypothesis tier, not clinical validation. 4) Remove MAP2K7 evidence or explicitly label it as off-pathway. 5) Add explicit prospective biomarker trial citation requirement before claiming predictive biomarker candidacy. 6) Quantify cardiac AE rates from trial publications rather than relying on raw FAERS counts.
critique.abstention_required: partial: OS benefit in adjuvant setting cannot be confirmed without mature OS data; predictive biomarker claims require abstention pending prospective validation
critique.claim_boundary: Supported: BRAF V600E as oncogenic driver, FDA approval of doublet regimens, RFS benefit in adjuvant stage III, mechanistic resistance taxonomy. Not supported: durable OS benefit in adjuvant setting, prospectively validated predictive biomarkers, clinical durability of triplet immunotherapy combinations.

report.title: BRAF V600E as a Clinically Precedented Oncogenic Driver in Metastatic and Adjuvant Melanoma
report.summary: BRAF V600E is a causally validated, clinically precedented oncogenic driver in melanoma, with FDA-approved combination regimens—dabrafenib plus trametinib and vemurafenib plus cobimetinib—targeting the MAP kinase/ERK signaling pathway. In the adjuvant stage III setting, the COMBI-AD trial demonstrated relapse-free survival and distant metastasis-free survival benefit for dabrafenib plus trametinib; overall survival data remain immature and OS benefit cannot be confirmed from available evidence. BRAF carries an Open Targets association score of 0.853 for melanoma, consistent with its established driver status. The primary unresolved scientific question concerns acquired resistance: MAPK pathway reactivation via BRAF amplification, splice variants, NRAS and MEK1/2 mutations, and paradoxical ERK activation are preclinically and retrospectively characterized mechanisms, but prospective validation in human tumors is incomplete and no approved strategy durably overcomes resistance. A second unresolved question involves combination sequencing with immunotherapy: transient tumor microenvironment remodeling provides a mechanistic hypothesis for triplet strategies, but clinical durability and tolerability lack Phase III validation. TMB and immune infiltration are candidate associative biomarkers for immunotherapy benefit, and PTEN loss associates with targeted therapy resistance, but none are prospectively validated predictive biomarkers in BRAF-mutant melanoma. The highest-priority next step is a prospective, biomarker-stratified trial distinguishing MAPK-driven from immune-mediated resistance mechanisms using longitudinal tumor biopsies.

biomedical_context.analysis_goal: Synthesize clinical evidence and mechanistic rationale for BRAF V600E targeting in melanoma by integrating: (1) approved BRAF/MEK inhibitor combination efficacy and unresolved sequencing/durability questions; (2) adaptive and acquired MAPK resistance mechanisms including BRAF amplification, NRAS mutation, MEK1/2 mutation, and paradoxical ERK activation; (3) tumor microenvironment contributions to resistance and the scientific basis for combining targeted therapy with anti-PD-1, anti-LAG-3, and triplet regimens; (4) predictive biomarkers for benefit versus intrinsic resistance including TMB and immune infiltration signatures; and (5) experimental validation strategies distinguishing MAPK-driven adaptive resistance from immune-mediated resistance to inform rational combination trial design.

The objective asks for experiments or validation.

case_profile.objective: Synthesize the scientific rationale and clinical evidence for BRAF V600E targeting in melanoma. Cover: (1) established clinical precedence for BRAF/MEK inhibitor combinations — dabrafenib plus trametinib, vemurafenib plus cobimetinib — distinguishing approved settings from ongoing questions about sequencing and durability; (2) adaptive and acquired resistance mechanisms including MAPK pathway reactivation via BRAF amplification, NRAS mutation, MEK1/2 mutation, and paradoxical ERK activation; (3) tumor microenvironment contributions to resistance and the rationale for immunotherapy combination — anti-PD-1, anti-LAG-3, and triplet strategies; (4) biomarkers predicting benefit versus intrinsic resistance including tumor mutational burden and immune infiltration signatures; and (5) validation experiments distinguishing MAPK-driven adaptive resistance from immune-mediated resistance that would inform combination trial design.

OpenTargets_get_disease_id_description_by_name returned OpenTargets search hits: melanoma: A malignant, usually aggressive tumor composed of atypical, neoplastic melanocytes. Most often, melanomas arise in the skin (cutaneous melanomas) and include the following histologic subtypes: superficial spreading melanoma, nodular melanoma, acral lentiginous melanoma, and lentigo maligna melanoma. Cutaneous melanomas may arise from acquired or congenital melanocytic or dysplastic nevi. Melanomas may also arise in other anatomic sites including the gastrointestinal system, eye, urinary tract, and reproductive system. Melanomas frequently metastasize to lymph nodes, liver, lungs, and brain.; cutaneous

{
  "title": "BRAF V600E as a Clinically Precedented Oncogenic Driver in Metastatic and Adjuvant Melanoma",
  "summary": "BRAF V600E is a causally validated, clinically precedented oncogenic driver in melanoma, with FDA-approved combination regimens—dabrafenib plus trametinib and vemurafenib plus cobimetinib—targeting the MAP kinase/ERK signaling pathway. In the adjuvant stage III setting, the COMBI-AD trial demonstrated relapse-free survival and distant metastasis-free survival benefit for dabrafenib plus trametinib; overall survival data remain immature and OS benefit cannot be confi

key_claims: BRAF V600E is the most frequently identified oncogenic mutation in melanoma and encodes a constitutively active serine/threonine kinase that drives MAP kinase/ERK pathway signaling, as established by NCBI Gene annotation and an Open Targets disease-association score of 0.853.

Dabrafenib (approved for melanoma across multiple indications per Open Targets) and trametinib are FDA-approved agents targeting the BRAF V600E-MEK-ERK axis, with adjuvant dabrafenib plus trametinib demonstrating relapse-free survival and distant metastasis-free survival benefit in stage III BRAF-mutant melanoma; mature overall survival data are not yet available.

Acquired resistance to BRAF-targeted therapy involves preclinically and retrospectively characterized MAPK reactivation mechanisms—including BRAF amplification, splice variants, and NRAS/MEK1/2 mutations—but prospective mechanistic validation in human tumor cohorts remains incomplete.

PTEN loss is associated with resistance to BRAF-targeted therapy in melanoma based on available evidence, but neither PTEN loss nor TMB nor immune infiltration signatures have been prospectively validated as predictive biomarkers for treatment selection in BRAF-mutant melanoma.

hits.name: melanoma
hits.description: A malignant, usually aggressive tumor composed of atypical, neoplastic melanocytes. Most often, melanomas arise in the skin (cutaneous melanomas) and include the following histologic subtypes: superficial spreading melanoma, nodular melanoma, acral lentiginous melanoma, and lentigo maligna melanoma. Cutaneous melanomas may arise from acquired or congenital melanocytic or dysplastic nevi. Melanomas may also arise in other anatomic sites including the gastrointestinal system, eye, urinary tract, and reproductive system. Melanomas frequently metastasize to lymph nodes, liver, lungs, and brain.

hits.name: cutaneous melanoma
hits.description: A primary melanoma arising from atypical melanocytes in the skin. Precursor lesions include acquired and congenital melanocytic nevi, and dysplastic nevi. Several histologic variants have been recognized, including superficial spreading melanoma, acral lentiginous melanoma, nodular melanoma, and lentigo maligna melanoma.

hits.name: Uveal Melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma derived from melanocytes of the uveal tract. It is the most common primary intraocular tumor in the United States and Western Europe. Similar to melanoma of the skin, it is rare in Africa and Asia. Diagnostic procedures include ophthalmoscopic exam, fluorescein angiography and ultrasound. Treatment includes: surgical excision of the eye, iridocyclectomy and tumor resection. Recent treatments also include radiotherapy or photo coagulation. Classification of uveal melanomas recognizes four cell types within these tumors: epithelioid, intermediate, mixed cell, and spindle cell types. The spindle cell type uveal melanomas are further sub-classified as spindle cell type A and spindle cell type B.

hits.name: melanoma-derived growth regulatory protein  level
hits.description: The amount of a melanoma-derived growth regulatory protein when measured in anatomical entity.

hits.name: superficial spreading melanoma
hits.description: A type of melanoma that typically occurs in light-skinned individuals ranging in age from young adults to the elderly. Risk factors include extensive sun exposure during childhood, a family history of melanoma, and the presence of dysplastic nevi.

hits.name: nodular melanoma
hits.description: An aggressive form of melanoma, frequently metastasizing to the lymph nodes. It presents as a papular or nodular raised skin lesion. It comprises approximately 10-15% of melanomas. Morphologically, it often displays an epithelioid appearance.

hits.name: retinal melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma affecting the retinal portion of the eye. --2003

hits.name: melanoma, cutaneous malignant, susceptibility to, 8
hits.description: An inherited cancer-predisposing syndrome due to a gain-of-function germline mutation in the MITF gene, associated with a higher incidence of amelanotic and nodular melanoma, multiple primary melanomas and increase in nevus number and size. It may also predispose to co-occurring melanoma and renal cell carcinoma and to pancreatic cancer.

hits.name: esophageal melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma affecting the esophageal wall. Melanoma in the esophagus is more commonly metastatic than primary. Primary melanomas of the esophagus are polypoid and clinically aggressive. (WHO, 2000)

hits.name: Non-Cutaneous Melanoma
hits.description: Melanoma is a malignant tumor of melanocytes, cells that are derived from the neural crest. Although most melanomas arise in the skin, they also may arise from mucosal surfaces or at other sites to which neural crest cells migrate. (PDQ)

hits.name: Ocular Melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma that arises from the structures of the eye or ocular adnexa.

hits.name: Spindle Cell Melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma characterized by the presence of malignant spindle-shaped melanocytes.

hits.name: acral lentiginous melanoma
hits.description: A form of melanoma occurring most often on the plantar, palmar, subungual, and periungual skin. It presents as a pigmented macular lesion with irregular borders. Morphologically, it consists of atypical spindled and dendritic melanocytes. The epidermis is often hyperplastic and there is pagetoid infiltration of the epidermis by anaplastic cells.

hits.name: digestive system melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma that arises from any part of the digestive system.

hits.name: level of melanoma-associated antigen B10 in blood serum
hits.description: The amount of a melanoma-associated antigen B10 when measured in blood serum.

hits.name: amelanotic melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma characterized by the complete absence of melanin pigment in the melanoma cells. It occurs more frequently on the face and it is often associated with desmoplastic reaction.

hits.name: Mixed Cell Uveal Melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma arising from the choroid, ciliary body, or the iris. It is characterized by the presence of a mixture of spindle A melanoma cells, spindle B melanoma cells, and epithelioid melanoma cells.

hits.name: Anal Melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma arising from the anus. Clinical presentation includes rectal bleeding, tenesmus, pain, and change in bowel habit. The prognosis is usually poor.

hits.name: choroid spindle cell melanoma
hits.description: A spindle cell melanoma that involves the optic choroid.

hits.name: metastatic melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma that has spread from its primary site to another anatomic site. Melanomas frequently metastasize to lymph nodes, liver, lungs, and brain.

hits.name: amelanotic skin melanoma
hits.description: An amelanotic melanoma that involves the zone of skin.

hits.name: Conjunctival Melanoma
hits.description: A malignant melanoma within the conjunctiva of the eye.

hits.name: desmoplastic melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma of the skin characterized by a proliferation of atypical spindled melanocytes in the dermis, in a background of abundant collagen. It usually presents as an amelanotic raised nodular lesion.

hits.name: Epithelioid Cell Uveal Melanoma
hits.description: A uveal melanoma characterized by the presence of malignant large epithelioid melanocytes.

hits.name: spindle cell intraocular melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma arising from the choroid, ciliary body, or the iris. It is characterized by the presence of spindle-shaped melanocytes.

hits.name: TRAMETINIB
hits.description: Small molecule drug with a maximum clinical stage of Approval (across all indications), with 3 approved and 32 investigational indications.

hits.name: TRAMETINIB DIMETHYL SULFOXIDE
hits.description: Small molecule drug with a maximum clinical stage of Approval (across all indications), with 6 approved and 3 investigational indications.

hits.name: RELATLIMAB

input.evidence_text: OpenTargets_get_disease_id_description_by_name returned OpenTargets search hits: melanoma: A malignant, usually aggressive tumor composed of atypical, neoplastic melanocytes. Most often, melanomas arise in the skin (cutaneous melanomas) and include the following histologic subtypes: superficial spreading melanoma, nodular melanoma, acral lentiginous melanoma, and lentigo maligna melanoma. Cutaneous melanomas may arise from acquired or congenital melanocytic or dysplastic nevi. Melanomas may also arise in other anatomic sites including the gastrointestinal system, eye, urinary tract, and reproductive system. Melanomas frequently metastasize to lymph nodes, liver, lungs, and brain.; cutaneous melanoma: A primary melanoma arising from atypical melanocytes in the skin. Precursor lesions include acquired and congenital melanocytic nevi, and dysplastic nevi. Several histologic variants have been recognized, including superficial spreading melanoma, acral lentiginous melanoma, nodular melanoma, and lentigo maligna melanoma.; Uveal Melanoma: A melanoma derived from melanocytes of the uveal tract. It is the most common primary intraocular tumor in the United States and Western Europe. Similar to melanoma of the skin, it is rare in Africa and Asia. Diagnostic procedures include ophthalmoscopic exam, fluorescein angiography and ultrasound. Treatment includes: surgical excision of the eye, iridocyclectomy and tumor resection. Recent treatments also include radiotherapy or photo coagulation. Classification of uveal melanomas recognizes four cell types within these tumors: epithelioid, intermediate, mixed cell, and spindle cell types. The spindle cell type uveal melanomas are further sub-classified as spindle cell type A and spindle cell type B.

hypothesis.title: BRAF clinical-precedence and mechanism review for melanoma
hypothesis.text: BRAF V600E is a causally validated, clinically precedented oncogenic driver in melanoma with FDA-approved combination therapies (dabrafenib+trametinib, vemurafenib+cobimetinib) demonstrating OS benefit in metastatic and adjuvant stage III settings. The outstanding scientific work concerns: (1) resistance biology — MAPK reactivation via BRAF amplification, splice variants, NRAS/MEK1/2 mutations, and paradoxical ERK activation are established mechanisms, but no approved strategy durably overcomes them; (2) combination sequencing with immunotherapy — transient TME remodeling provides mechanistic rationale for triplet strategies, but clinical validation of durability and tolerability remains incomplete; (3) biomarker-guided patient selection — TMB and immune infiltration signatures are candidate associative biomarkers for immunotherapy benefit, and PTEN loss/MITF amplification associate with targeted therapy resistance, but none are prospectively validated predictive biomarkers in this specific context. Priority experiments include prospective biomarker-stratified trials distinguishing MAPK-driven from immune-mediated resistance, longitudinal TME profiling to define optimal immunotherapy combination windows, and dedicated safety studies for triplet regimens in patients with baseline cardiac dysfunction.

evidence.text: NCBI Gene BRAF: This gene encodes a protein belonging to the RAF family of serine/threonine protein kinases. This protein plays a role in regulating the MAP kinase/ERK signaling pathway, which affects cell division, differentiation, and secretion. Mutations in this gene, most commonly the V600E mutation, are the most frequently identified cancer-causing mutations in melanoma, and have been identified in various other cancers as well, including non-Hodgkin lymphoma, colorectal cancer, thyroid carcinoma, non-small cell lung carcinoma, hairy cell leukemia and adenocarcinoma of lung. Mutations in this gene are also associated with cardiofaciocutaneous, Noonan, and Costello syndromes, which exhibit overlapping phenotypes. A pseudogene of this gene has been identified on the X chromosome. [provided by RefSeq, Aug 2017]

evidence.text: NCBI Gene MAP2K7: The protein encoded by this gene is a dual specificity protein kinase that belongs to the MAP kinase kinase family. This kinase specifically activates MAPK8/JNK1 and MAPK9/JNK2, and this kinase itself is phosphorylated and activated by MAP kinase kinase kinases including MAP3K1/MEKK1, MAP3K2/MEKK2,MAP3K3/MEKK5, and MAP4K2/GCK. This kinase is involved in the signal transduction mediating the cell responses to proinflammatory cytokines, and environmental stresses. Alternative splicing results in multiple transcript variants. [provided by RefSeq, Jul 2014]

evidence.text: OpenTargets_get_disease_id_description_by_name returned OpenTargets search hits: melanoma: A malignant, usually aggressive tumor composed of atypical, neoplastic melanocytes. Most often, melanomas arise in the skin (cutaneous melanomas) and include the following histologic subtypes: superficial spreading melanoma, nodular melanoma, acral lentiginous melanoma, and lentigo maligna melanoma. Cutaneous melanomas may arise from acquired or congenital melanocytic or dysplastic nevi. Melanomas may also arise in other anatomic sites including the gastrointestinal system, eye, urinary tract, and reproductive system. Melanomas frequently metastasize to lymph nodes, liver, lungs, and brain.; cutaneous melanoma: A primary melanoma arising from atypical melanocytes in the skin. Precursor lesions include acquired and congenital melanocytic nevi, and dysplastic nevi. Several histologic variants have been recognized, including superficial spreading melanoma, acral lentiginous melanoma, nodular melanoma, and lentigo maligna melanoma.; Uveal Melanoma: A melanoma derived from melanocytes of the uveal tract. It is the most common primary intraocular tumor in the United States and Western Europe. Similar to melanoma of the skin, it is rare in Africa and Asia. Diagnostic procedures include ophthalmoscopic exam, fluorescein angiography and ultrasound. Treatment includes: surgical excision of the eye, iridocyclectomy and tumor resection. Recent treatments also include radiotherapy or photo coagulation. Classification of uveal melanomas recognizes four cell types within these tumors: epithelioid, intermediate, mixed cell, and spindle cell types. The spindle cell type uveal melanomas are further sub-classified as spindle cell type A and spindle cell type B.

hits.name: melanoma-derived growth regulatory protein  level
hits.description: The amount of a melanoma-derived growth regulatory protein when measured in anatomical entity.

hits.name: superficial spreading melanoma
hits.description: A type of melanoma that typically occurs in light-skinned individuals ranging in age from young adults to the elderly. Risk factors include extensive sun exposure during childhood, a family history of melanoma, and the presence of dysplastic nevi.

hits.name: nodular melanoma
hits.description: An aggressive form of melanoma, frequently metastasizing to the lymph nodes. It presents as a papular or nodular raised skin lesion. It comprises approximately 10-15% of melanomas. Morphologically, it often displays an epithelioid appearance.

hits.name: retinal melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma affecting the retinal portion of the eye. --2003

hits.name: melanoma, cutaneous malignant, susceptibility to, 8
hits.description: An inherited cancer-predisposing syndrome due to a gain-of-function germline mutation in the MITF gene, associated with a higher incidence of amelanotic and nodular melanoma, multiple primary melanomas and increase in nevus number and size. It may also predispose to co-occurring melanoma and renal cell carcinoma and to pancreatic cancer.

hits.name: esophageal melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma affecting the esophageal wall. Melanoma in the esophagus is more commonly metastatic than primary. Primary melanomas of the esophagus are polypoid and clinically aggressive. (WHO, 2000)

hits.name: Non-Cutaneous Melanoma
hits.description: Melanoma is a malignant tumor of melanocytes, cells that are derived from the neural crest. Although most melanomas arise in the skin, they also may arise from mucosal surfaces or at other sites to which neural crest cells migrate. (PDQ)

hits.name: Ocular Melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma that arises from the structures of the eye or ocular adnexa.

hits.name: Spindle Cell Melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma characterized by the presence of malignant spindle-shaped melanocytes.

hits.name: acral lentiginous melanoma
hits.description: A form of melanoma occurring most often on the plantar, palmar, subungual, and periungual skin. It presents as a pigmented macular lesion with irregular borders. Morphologically, it consists of atypical spindled and dendritic melanocytes. The epidermis is often hyperplastic and there is pagetoid infiltration of the epidermis by anaplastic cells.

hits.name: digestive system melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma that arises from any part of the digestive system.

hits.name: level of melanoma-associated antigen B10 in blood serum
hits.description: The amount of a melanoma-associated antigen B10 when measured in blood serum.

hits.name: amelanotic melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma characterized by the complete absence of melanin pigment in the melanoma cells. It occurs more frequently on the face and it is often associated with desmoplastic reaction.

hits.name: Mixed Cell Uveal Melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma arising from the choroid, ciliary body, or the iris. It is characterized by the presence of a mixture of spindle A melanoma cells, spindle B melanoma cells, and epithelioid melanoma cells.

hits.name: Anal Melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma arising from the anus. Clinical presentation includes rectal bleeding, tenesmus, pain, and change in bowel habit. The prognosis is usually poor.

hits.name: choroid spindle cell melanoma
hits.description: A spindle cell melanoma that involves the optic choroid.

hits.name: metastatic melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma that has spread from its primary site to another anatomic site. Melanomas frequently metastasize to lymph nodes, liver, lungs, and brain.

hits.name: amelanotic skin melanoma
hits.description: An amelanotic melanoma that involves the zone of skin.

hits.name: Conjunctival Melanoma
hits.description: A malignant melanoma within the conjunctiva of the eye.

hits.name: desmoplastic melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma of the skin characterized by a proliferation of atypical spindled melanocytes in the dermis, in a background of abundant collagen. It usually presents as an amelanotic raised nodular lesion.

hits.name: Epithelioid Cell Uveal Melanoma
hits.description: A uveal melanoma characterized by the presence of malignant large epithelioid melanocytes.

hits.name: spindle cell intraocular melanoma
hits.description: A melanoma arising from the choroid, ciliary body, or the iris. It is characterized by the presence of spindle-shaped melanocytes.

content.title: BRAF clinical-precedence and mechanism review for melanoma
content.hypothesis: BRAF V600E is a causally validated, clinically precedented oncogenic driver in melanoma with FDA-approved combination therapies (dabrafenib+trametinib, vemurafenib+cobimetinib) demonstrating OS benefit in metastatic and adjuvant stage III settings. The outstanding scientific work concerns: (1) resistance biology — MAPK reactivation via BRAF amplification, splice variants, NRAS/MEK1/2 mutations, and paradoxical ERK activation are established mechanisms, but no approved strategy durably overcomes them; (2) combination sequencing with immunotherapy — transient TME remodeling provides mechanistic rationale for triplet strategies, but clinical validation of durability and tolerability remains incomplete; (3) biomarker-guided patient selection — TMB and immune infiltration signatures are candidate associative biomarkers for immunotherapy benefit, and PTEN loss/MITF amplification associate with targeted therapy resistance, but none are prospectively validated predictive biomarkers in this specific context. Priority experiments include prospective biomarker-stratified trials distinguishing MAPK-driven from immune-mediated resistance, longitudinal TME profiling to define optimal immunotherapy combination windows, and dedicated safety studies for triplet regimens in patients with baseline cardiac dysfunction.

evidence.text: NCBI Gene BRAF: This gene encodes a protein belonging to the RAF family of serine/threonine protein kinases. This protein plays a role in regulating the MAP kinase/ERK signaling pathway, which affects cell division, differentiation, and secretion. Mutations in this gene, most commonly the V600E mutation, are the most frequently identified cancer-causing mutations in melanoma, and have been identified in various other cancers as well, including non-Hodgkin lymphoma, colorectal cancer, thyroid carcinoma, non-small cell lung carcinoma, hairy cell leukemia and adenocarcinoma of lung. Mutations in this gene are also associated with cardiofaciocutaneous, Noonan, and Costello syndromes, which exhibit overlapping phenotypes. A pseudogene of this gene has been identified on the X chromosome. [provided by RefSeq, Aug 2017]

evidence.text: NCBI Gene MAP2K7: The protein encoded by this gene is a dual specificity protein kinase that belongs to the MAP kinase kinase family. This kinase specifically activates MAPK8/JNK1 and MAPK9/JNK2, and this kinase itself is phosphorylated and activated by MAP kinase kinase kinases including MAP3K1/MEKK1, MAP3K2/MEKK2,MAP3K3/MEKK5, and MAP4K2/GCK. This kinase is involved in the signal transduction mediating the cell responses to proinflammatory cytokines, and environmental stresses. Alternative splicing results in multiple transcript variants. [provided by RefSeq, Jul 2014]

evidence.text: OpenTargets_get_disease_id_description_by_name returned OpenTargets search hits: melanoma: A malignant, usually aggressive tumor composed of atypical, neoplastic melanocytes. Most often, melanomas arise in the skin (cutaneous melanomas) and include the following histologic subtypes: superficial spreading melanoma, nodular melanoma, acral lentiginous melanoma, and lentigo maligna melanoma. Cutaneous melanomas may arise from acquired or congenital melanocytic or dysplastic nevi. Melanomas may also arise in other anatomic sites including the gastrointestinal system, eye, urinary tract, and reproductive system. Melanomas frequently metastasize to lymph nodes, liver, lungs, and brain.; cutaneous melanoma: A primary melanoma arising from atypical melanocytes in the skin. Precursor lesions include acquired and congenital melanocytic nevi, and dysplastic nevi. Several histologic variants have been recognized, including superficial spreading melanoma, acral lentiginous melanoma, nodular melanoma, and lentigo maligna melanoma.; Uveal Melanoma: A melanoma derived from melanocytes of the uveal tract. It is the most common primary intraocular tumor in the United States and Western Europe. Similar to melanoma of the skin, it is rare in Africa and Asia. Diagnostic procedures include ophthalmoscopic exam, fluorescein angiography and ultrasound. Treatment includes: surgical excision of the eye, iridocyclectomy and tumor resection. Recent treatments also include radiotherapy or photo coagulation. Classification of uveal melanomas recognizes four cell types within these tumors: epithelioid, intermediate, mixed cell, and spindle cell types. The spindle cell type uveal melanomas are further sub-classified as spindle cell type A and spindle cell type B.
